reforms.description: The Mexican government has initiated a significant reform aimed at boosting R&D and infrastructure, with plans to double R&D spending from 0.54% of GDP to 1%. This initiative is designed to enhance the country's innovative potential and competitiveness in global value chains.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been put into action as part of the government's commitment to enhance R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in R&D funding policy.
reforms.source_quote: Raising R&D intensity further is one of the priorities of the current administration, which intends to double R&D spending from the current 0.54% of GDP to 1%.

reforms.description: The Mexican government has introduced special economic zones (SEZs) to support development in less developed states, attract investment, and improve infrastructure while reducing regulatory barriers. Additionally, measures have been taken to promote high-tech industrial clusters linked to universities and early-stage investment vehicles, including the establishment of the Simplified Actions Corporation (SAS) to facilitate startup creation.
reforms.component_name: Special Economic Zones and Startup Support
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of SEZs and support for startups is expected to foster innovation and economic growth in underdeveloped regions.
reforms.status_evidence: The SEZs and related measures have been actively implemented to support economic development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of SEZs represents a major structural change in economic policy aimed at fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Mexican Government created a new figure through the Commercial Corporations Law: the Simplified Actions Corporation, which allows firms to be created within 24 hours through a digital platform.

reforms.description: A tax credit on R&D was approved by Congress, allowing firms to offset 30% of their qualifying R&D expenditures against income tax, aimed at incentivizing private sector investment in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit is designed to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been enacted and is currently in effect for qualifying firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the tax credit is a narrower reform compared to broader R&D funding initiatives.
reforms.source_quote: A tax credit on R&D will start in 2017, which will allow participating firms a 30% tax credit on their R&D expenditures.
